summary: redis-type-os impersonates redis-om and pulls a typosquat dependency on install
details: Package name 'redis-type-os' is a near-edit of the legitimate 'redis-om' npm package. package.json claims repository 'github:redis/redis-om-node', homepage 'https://github.com/redis/redis-om-node#readme', and author 'Guy Royse <guy@guyroyse.com>' — identifiers belonging to the real redis-om maintainer, who does not own this name. The dist/ bundle, README, docs/, and CHANGELOG are copied verbatim from redis-om-node so the package looks legitimate on inspection. package.json:64 declares a dependency on 'index-ulid@^3.0.2', a typosquat of the real 'ulid' package. The runtime bundle dist/index.js (line 1842) already inlines the legitimate ulid source, so 'index-ulid' is never required by this package's code — its only effect is to be transitively fetched by npm install, exposing the installer to whatever code that sibling package contains.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
